memberdef.name: SHELL_DUMMY_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static struct shell_dummy _name##_shell_dummy; \ struct shell_transport _name = { \ .api = &shell_dummy_transport_api, \ .ctx = (struct shell_dummy *)&_name##_shell_dummy \ }

briefdescription.para: This function shall not be used directly.

detaileddescription.para: It provides pointer to shell dummy backend instance.
Function returns pointer to the shell dummy instance. This instance can be next used with shell_execute_cmd function in order to test commands behavior.

briefdescription.para: Returns the buffered output in the shell and resets the pointer.

detaileddescription.para: The returned data is always followed by a nul character at position *sizep

parameterdescription.para: Returns size of data in shell buffer

simplesect.para: pointer to buffer containing shell output

briefdescription.para: Clears the output buffer in the shell backend.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, const char *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: shell_backend_dummy_push_input

briefdescription.para: Push input data to the dummy shell backend.

detaileddescription.para: This function queues input data that will be returned by subsequent read operations. Useful for testing shell input handling.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -ENOMEM if buffer is full

briefdescription.para: Clear the input buffer in the shell backend.
